echo.description: Writes its arguments to standard output separated by spaces followed by a newline. The -n flag suppresses the trailing newline, -e enables backslash escape interpretation.

test.description: Evaluates conditional expressions for file attributes, string comparisons, and arithmetic tests, returning exit status 0 for true and 1 for false, used in if statements and loops.

read.signature: read [-r] [-p prompt] [-t timeout] [-n nchars] [-d delim] [-a array] [name ...]
read.description: Reads a line of input from standard input and splits it into fields assigned to named variables, with -r preventing backslash escape processing and -p displaying a prompt.

trap.description: Registers a command to execute when the shell receives specified signals such as EXIT, INT, TERM, or HUP, enabling cleanup handlers and graceful shutdown procedures.

set.description: Sets or unsets shell options and positional parameters. Common flags include -e (exit on error), -u (treat unset variables as error), -x (print commands before execution).

export.description: Marks variables for automatic export to the environment of subsequently executed child processes, making them available as environment variables in subshells and commands.

getopts.description: Parses positional parameters as command-line options according to the optstring specification, setting the named variable to each option found and OPTARG to its argument.

shift.description: Shifts positional parameters to the left by n positions (default 1), discarding the first n parameters and renumbering the remaining ones starting from $1 for argument processing.

eval.description: Concatenates its arguments into a single command string and then executes the result as a shell command, enabling dynamic command construction and variable indirection patterns.

exec.description: Replaces the current shell process with the specified command without creating a new process, or with no command applies redirections to the current shell for file descriptor manipulation.

source.description: Reads and executes commands from the specified file in the current shell environment, making all variable assignments and function definitions persist in the calling shell.

printf.description: Formats and outputs text according to a format string with conversion specifiers like %s, %d, %f, providing more precise control over output formatting than echo.

local.description: Declares variables with local scope within a function, preventing modifications from affecting the calling scope, essential for writing reusable shell functions without side effects.

wait.description: Suspends execution until specified background processes or jobs complete and returns their exit status, or waits for all background children when called without arguments.

declare.description: Declares variables with specific attributes such as -i for integer, -a for indexed array, -A for associative array, -r for readonly, and -x for export in bash scripts.
